# 1. Overview

# 2. Getting Started

::: panel-tabset
## Installing and loading libraries


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# | fig_width: 12
# | fig_height: 8
pacman::p_load(ggrepel, patchwork, 
               ggthemes, hrbrthemes,
               tidyverse) 
```
:::


## Importing data


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
exam_data <- read_csv("data/Exam_data.csv")
```
:::

:::

::: panel-tabset
## Data summary


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
summary(exam_data)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```

```


:::
:::

glimpse(exam_data)

:::
:::

:::

# 3. Beyond ggplot2: ggrepel


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x= MATHS, 
           y=ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method=lm, 
              linewidth=0.5) +  
  geom_label(aes(label = ID), 
             hjust = .5, 
             vjust = -.5) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100),
                  ylim=c(0,100)) +
  ggtitle("English scores versus Math scores for Primary 3")
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](HO_EX02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-6-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## 3.1 Working with ggrepel


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x= MATHS, 
           y=ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method=lm, 
              size=0.5) +  
  geom_label_repel(aes(label = ID), 
                   fontface = "bold") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100),
                  ylim=c(0,100)) +
  ggtitle("English scores versus Math scores for Primary 3")
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](HO_EX02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-7-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


# 4. Beyond ggplot2: Themes


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(data=exam_data, 
             aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=20, 
                 boundary = 100,
                 color="grey25", 
                 fill="grey90") +
  theme_gray() +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Maths scores") 
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](HO_EX02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-8-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


::: panel-tabset
## ggthemes


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(data=exam_data, 
             aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=20, 
                 boundary = 100,
                 color="grey25", 
                 fill="grey90") +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Maths scores") +
  theme_economist()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](HO_EX02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-9-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## hrbthemes


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(data=exam_data, 
             aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=20, 
                 boundary = 100,
                 color="grey25", 
                 fill="grey90") +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Maths scores") +
  theme_ipsum()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](HO_EX02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-10-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## hrbthemes - ipsum


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(data=exam_data, 
             aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=20, 
                 boundary = 100,
                 color="grey25", 
                 fill="grey90") +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Maths scores") +
  theme_ipsum(axis_title_size = 18,
              base_size = 15,
              grid = "Y")
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](HO_EX02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-11-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::

:::

# 5. Beyond Single Graph

::: panel-tabset
## Math Scores


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
p1 <- ggplot(data=exam_data, 
             aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=20, 
                 boundary = 100,
                 color="grey25", 
                 fill="grey90") + 
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100)) +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Math scores")

p1
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](HO_EX02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-12-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## English Scores


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
p2 <- ggplot(data=exam_data, 
             aes(x = ENGLISH)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=20, 
                 boundary = 100,
                 color="grey25", 
                 fill="grey90") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100)) +
  ggtitle("Distribution of English scores")

p2
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](HO_EX02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-13-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## Math vs English


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
p3 <- ggplot(data=exam_data, 
             aes(x= MATHS, 
                 y=ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method=lm, 
              size=0.5) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100),
                  ylim=c(0,100)) +
  ggtitle("English scores versus Math scores for Primary 3")

p3
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](HO_EX02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-14-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::

:::

## 5.1 Creating Composite Graphics: pathwork methods

## 5.2 Combining two ggplot2 graphs

::: panel-tabset
## Horizontal


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
p1 + p2
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](HO_EX02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-15-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## Vertical


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
p1 / p2
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](HO_EX02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-16-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## Relative area


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
p1 + p2 + plot_layout(ncol=2,widths=c(2,1))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](HO_EX02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-17-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::

:::

## 5.3 Combining three ggplot2 graphs

::: panel-tabset
## / and \| operators


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
(p1 / p2) | p3
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](HO_EX02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-18-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## - operator (subtraction)


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
p1 + p2 - p3 + plot_layout(ncol=1)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](HO_EX02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-19-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## Nested layouts


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
p3 + {
  p1 + p2 + plot_layout(ncol=1)
}
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](HO_EX02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-20-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## With non-ggplot content


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
((p1 / p2) | p3) + grid:: textGrob('(Can add content here.)', hjust=0, x=-0, gp=grid::gpar(font=3, fontsize = 12))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](HO_EX02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-21-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::

:::

## 5.4 Creating a composite figure with tag

::: panel-tabset
## Enumeration


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
((p1 / p2) | p3) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'I')
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](HO_EX02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-22-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## With tagging


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
((p1 / p2) | p3)  + 
  plot_layout(tag_level = 'new') +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = c('A', '1'), tag_prefix = 'Fig. ', tag_sep = '.', 
                  tag_suffix = ':')
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](HO_EX02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-23-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::

:::

## 5.5 Creating figure with inset


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
p4 <- ggplot(data=exam_data, 
             aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=20, 
                 boundary = 100,
                 color="grey25", 
                 fill="grey90") + 
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100)) +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Math scores")

p5 <- ggplot(data=exam_data, 
             aes(x = ENGLISH)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=20, 
                 boundary = 100,
                 color="grey25", 
                 fill="grey90") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100)) +
  ggtitle("Distribution of English scores")

p6 <- ggplot(data=exam_data, 
             aes(x= MATHS, 
                 y=ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method=lm, 
              size=0.5) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100),
                  ylim=c(0,100)) +
  ggtitle("Correlation between English & Math scores")

p6 +  inset_element(p5,
                    left = 0.02,
                    bottom=0.7,
                    right= 0.5,
                    top=1)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](HO_EX02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-24-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## 5.6 Composite figure: Patchwork + ggtheme

::: panel-tabset
## theme_economist()


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
patchwork <- ((p4/p5) | p6)

patchwork & theme_economist() +
  theme(plot.title=element_text(size =10),
                                        axis.title.y=element_text(size = 9,
                                                                  angle = 0,
                                                                  vjust=0.9),
                                         axis.title.x=element_text(size = 9))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](HO_EX02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-25-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## theme_solarized


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
patchwork & theme_solarized() +
  theme(plot.title=element_text(size =10),
                                        axis.title.y=element_text(size = 9,
                                                                  angle = 0,
                                                                  vjust=0.9),
                                         axis.title.x=element_text(size = 9))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](HO_EX02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-26-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::

:::

# 6. References

-   [Patchwork R package goes nerd viral](https://www.littlemissdata.com/blog/patchwork)
-   [ggrepel](https://ggrepel.slowkow.com/)
-   [ggthemes](https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/reference/ggtheme.html)
-   [hrbrthemes](https://cinc.rud.is/web/packages/hrbrthemes/)
-   [ggplot tips: Arranging plots](https://albert-rapp.de/post/2021-10-28-extend-plot-variety/)
-   [ggplot2 Theme Elements Demonstration](https://henrywang.nl/ggplot2-theme-elements-demonstration/)
-   [ggplot2 Theme Elements Reference Sheet](https://isabella-b.com/blog/ggplot2-theme-elements-reference/)
